content_markdown:
The circuit shows a resistor $R$ connected in series with a battery and a resistor of resistance $10\,\Omega$. The emf of the battery is $20\text{V}$ and it has negligible internal resistance. The current in the circuit is $1.0\text{A}$.

Which of the following is the resistance of $R$?

diagram_metadata.description: A series circuit containing a battery of 20V, a resistor of 10 ohms, and a resistor R. An ammeter reading or current arrow indicates 1.0A.

vibe_explanation: Using Ohm's Law for the whole circuit, $V = I \times R_{total}$. Given $V = 20\text{V}$ and $I = 1.0\text{A}$, the total resistance $R_{total}$ must be $20\,\Omega$. Since the resistors are in series, $R_{total} = R + 10\,\Omega$. Therefore, $R = 20\,\Omega - 10\,\Omega = 10\,\Omega$.
